entries.title: Claude in Excel: Building Financial Models Directly in Spreadsheets
entries.summary: You can now prototype and iterate financial models faster by using Claude natively in Excel rather than copying formulas back and forth between an AI chat interface and your spreadsheet.

entries.title: Power Query for Finance Automation: One-Click Dashboard Refresh
entries.summary: Switch to Power Query for financial consolidation to turn a 3-hour manual monthly task into a 10-second refresh, reducing errors and freeing time for actual analysis.

entries.title: Efficiency Gains from Claude in Excel: Reducing 3-Statement Model Time to 90 Minutes
entries.summary: Claude's ability to trace cell references and generate forecasts with formulas intact can cut your financial modeling time roughly in half, freeing you for higher-level analysis.

entries.title: ChatGPT Power Query Automation for FP&A: Generating M Code Without Manual Editing
entries.summary: You can automate complex data cleaning and transformation tasks in Excel by prompting ChatGPT to generate Power Query M code, eliminating manual transformation work.

entries.title: ChatGPT for FP&A Workflows: Automating Variance Analysis, Forecasting, and Reports
entries.summary: Structure ChatGPT requests as an FP&A analyst taking on specific jobs (variance analysis, consolidation, narrative), and feed it your actual Excel data to automate repetitive reporting in minutes rather than hours.

entries.title: Board Forecast Presentation: Visual Storytelling and Multi-Scenario Planning
entries.summary: Structure board forecast presentations around a clear decision or outcome rather than information dump, and pre-wire key stakeholders to surface objections before the meeting.

entries.title: CFO Lessons: Presenting Forecasts to the Board—Lead with Impact, Not Context
entries.summary: Start your board forecast presentation with the financial implications and ask, then support with assumptions, scenarios, and risks—respecting the board's time and preference for impact-driven storytelling.

entries.title: Managing Difficult Conversations with Business Partners: Preparation and Collaborative Framing
entries.summary: Before a tough conversation with a business partner about budget or spending, identify the root financial problem, define what 'win' looks like for both sides, and hold it live to read tone and build collaborative momentum.

entries.title: Pushing Back on Unrealistic Budget Requests: Ground Your Objection in Data and Propose Win-Win Alternatives
entries.summary: Ground your budget pushback in specific data and benchmarks, ask clarifying questions to understand your partner's real objective, and propose a win-win alternative rather than simply rejecting their request.

entries.title: Headcount Planning for the 2026 Budget Cycle: A Structured Four-Step Approach
entries.summary: Structure your headcount planning in four steps: assess current state, forecast demand by business goal, analyze gaps, and build scenarios—allowing you to align staffing with strategic objectives and present options to leadership.
